steps.title: Upload multiple visual references
steps.description: Provide separate images for subject, pose, style, environment, or color palette so each reference has a clear role.

steps.title: Specify which elements should carry over
steps.description: Use a short text description to explain what you want from each image—for example outfit from one, pose from another, and lighting from a third.

steps.title: Generate AI images that respect all inputs
steps.description: Run the Multi References workflow to fuse your inputs into coherent visuals that honor your constraints and keep key details aligned.

steps.title: Refine and export for production or exploration
steps.description: Iterate on your setup, adjust references or instructions, and export the best results for concept art, campaigns, or as inputs to other workflows.

items.question: What does the Multi References AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow lets you generate new images that blend multiple visual references—such as subject, pose, style, or environment—into a single, coherent output. It is designed as a multi reference image generator for teams that need precise visual control.

items.question: What kinds of reference images should I prepare?
items.answer: Use clear images that each represent something specific you care about: one for pose, one for outfit, one for lighting, one for environment, or existing brand visuals. The more focused each reference is, the easier it is to tell the workflow which parts to keep.

items.question: How do I tell the workflow what to keep from each reference?
items.answer: Alongside your uploads, use text instructions to specify what should carry over from each image—for example "use the pose from reference A, the outfit and colors from reference B, and the lighting and background from reference C." This helps the AI respect your constraints when combining references.

items.question: What are typical use cases for multi-reference image generation?
items.answer: Common use cases include building consistent character or costume designs from mixed references, keeping campaign creatives on-brand by grounding them in existing assets, and visualizing products in new environments while retaining the original product look.

items.question: Can I use Multi References to keep a character or product consistent across many images?
items.answer: You can reuse the same identity or product references across multiple runs so core attributes stay aligned while you vary poses, environments, or styling. This is especially useful in character pipelines or multi-asset campaigns.

items.question: How do credits and costs work for Multi References generations?
items.answer: Each generation consumes credits based on the model and settings you use. A good approach is to test with a few shorter or lower-cost generations first, then spend more credits on higher-quality runs once you are confident about your reference setup.
